description_long: This is an irregularly shaped, medium-sized stone with a rugged, angular profile and a predominantly grayish-brown coloration. The surface displays a mix of matte and rough textures, with visible flake scars and chipped edges, suggesting it may have been shaped or struck. Patches of lighter tan and orange hues are scattered across the stone, giving it a mottled appearance. One side features a smoother, flatter area with some faint markings or inscriptions, while the opposite side is more jagged and uneven. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or openings, and its overall form is reminiscent of a hand-sized cobble or tool. The weathering is moderate, with some surface smoothing but still retaining sharp edges and fresh-looking breaks.
description: A rugged, angular stone with gray, brown, and tan mottling, featuring chipped edges and a rough, matte surface.
description_view_0: This view shows the broadest face of the stone, highlighting its mottled gray and tan coloration and the uneven, chipped surface.
description_view_1: A narrow, edge-on view reveals the stone's thickness and the sharp, angular profile of its sides.
description_view_2: The opposite broad face displays a smoother, flatter area with visible markings and lighter coloration, contrasting with the rougher edges.
description_view_3: Another edge-on view, similar to view 1, emphasizing the stone's jagged, elongated form and layered color transitions.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or scattered among other stones
attribute_pattern: Mottled coloration with irregular patches of tan and orange, and visible flake scars from chipping
attribute_weathering: Moderately weathered with some surface smoothing and sharp, fresh-looking breaks
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil and other stones, but contrasts with paved or manicured urban surfaces
